channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Darren Till wins BKFC debut with one-punch knockout over Aaron Chalmers
item.description: In his BKFC debut, former UFC contender Darren Till faced Aaron Chalmers in a high-stakes bare-knuckle boxing match at BKFC 90. The fight began with Chalmers taking control, landing a sharp left hook ...

item.title: Tanzania plans stricter regulations on shisha amid concerns over drug misuse
item.description: The Government of Tanzania is preparing new legislation aimed at tightening control over the use of shisha following growing concerns that the product is being misused for consuming narcotic substance...

item.title: Meteor Breaks Apart Over Northeastern US, Producing Significant Explosion
item.description: A meteor traveling at over 120,000 kilometers per hour exploded over the northeastern United States on Saturday, creating a blast equivalent to 300 tons of TNT. NASA confirmed the fireball disintegrat...

item.title: NCR Secures 19th Consecutive Overall Title at 2026 Palarong Pambansa
item.description: The 2026 Palarong Pambansa, held in Agusan del Sur, concluded on May 31 with the National Capital Region (NCR) once again dominating the national school-based sports competition. NCR secured its 19th ...

item.title: President Ruto Launches Development Projects in Northern Kenya Ahead of Madaraka Day
item.description: President William Ruto started a three-day tour of northern Kenya on Sunday, visiting Wajir County as part of preparations for the Madaraka Day celebrations in Wajir town. His first stop was Wajir Sou...

item.title: Federal workers’ forum threatens nationwide strike over wage disputes and rising insecurity
item.description: The Federal Workers Forum (FWF) has issued a strong warning that it may mobilise federal civil servants for an indefinite nationwide strike over unresolved wage issues and worsening insecurity in Nige...

item.title: Tim Allen and Tom Hanks Make Surprise Appearance at Soccer Aid 2026
item.description: During the 2026 Soccer Aid charity match, actor Tim Allen caused a stir when he swore live on air while appearing alongside Tom Hanks. The event, celebrating its twentieth anniversary, has raised over...

item.title: Analysis of whether President Ramaphosa’s reforms in South Africa will endure beyond his presidency
item.description: This article reflects on the political and economic legacy of South African President Cyril Ramaphosa as Parliament prepares for the first meeting of an impeachment committee. Although the possibility...

item.title: Tony Abbott’s return as Liberal Party president raises risks for Angus Taylor’s leadership
item.description: Tony Abbott’s unopposed appointment as federal president of the Liberal Party has sparked concern about its potential impact on current opposition leader Angus Taylor. Traditionally a behind-the-scene...

item.title: Families of former Philippine senators condemn current Senate over alleged misconduct and controversies
item.description: Relatives of prominent Philippine senators who served before the Martial Law era issued a rare collective statement strongly criticising the current Senate, expressing deep concern over what they desc...

item.title: Chadema Faces Internal Conflicts Amid Political Challenges
item.description: One of the Chadema party registrars has openly revealed internal divisions inside the party, which is Tanzania’s main opposition. The disclosure shows tensions that have been brewing for some time, an...

item.title: Allegations of substandard care raised following the death of stroke patient at Tygerberg Hospice
item.description: Friends of Johan Duvenhage, a 66-year-old former lawyer and finance professional, have raised serious concerns about the quality of care he received during his final weeks at a Tygerberg Hospice-linke...

item.title: Fuel price pressures and rising interest rates raise fairness and mental health challenges for South African employers
item.description: South African employers are facing growing pressure as rising fuel costs and higher borrowing rates from the South African Reserve Bank place additional strain on households and the broader economy. T...

item.title: EU plans to reduce reliance on US tech with new rules on chips, cloud and AI
item.description: The European Union is preparing a major policy package aimed at reducing its dependence on American technology companies and Chinese semiconductor supply chains. The proposed measures focus on key sec...

item.title: PM Modi and Pope highlight importance of human-centric and ethical AI governance
item.description: Amid the rapid global race to dominate artificial intelligence technologies, a broader international debate is emerging on ensuring that AI remains aligned with human values, ethics, and public welfar...

item.title: CCTV evidence reveals student involvement in Utumishi Girls’ dormitory fire that killed 16
item.description: CCTV footage has emerged showing disturbing details of how a dormitory fire at Utumishi Girls’ School was allegedly started by a group of students. According to reports from the Directorate of Crimina...

item.title: SSS arrests five alleged arms couriers linked to Niger school abduction case
item.description: The State Security Service (SSS) in Nigeria has arrested five suspects believed to be involved as arms couriers and logistics support operatives linked to the mass abduction of schoolchildren in Niger...

item.title: Readers Share Their Choices for Explaining Music to an Alien
item.description: The Guardian's long-running Notes & Queries series invited readers to imagine what they would play if an alien landed and asked them, 'What is music?' Participants are encouraged to select pieces that...

item.title: B.C. Conservatives present united caucus following Kerry-Lynne Findlay leadership win
item.description: The British Columbia Conservative caucus sought to project unity less than 24 hours after electing Kerry-Lynne Findlay as its new leader, despite a leadership race that exposed internal divisions with...

item.title: President Marcos Jr. discusses bilateral matters in phone call with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio
item.description: President Ferdinand “Bongbong” Marcos Jr. had a phone conversation with United States Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Monday morning, as confirmed by the Presidential Communications Office (PCO). Th...
